security demonstration \u2014 it's a clarity         demonstration. It explicitly maps out what the defense does         and doesn't claim."},"test_ForwardSecurity_PastPreimagesUselessAfterAdvance()":{"details":"The cryptographic claim: an attacker who later obtains a chain         preimage cannot use it to forge an upgrade if the chain has         already advanced past that preimage's position.         Past preimages are PUBLICLY VISIBLE after a successful upgrade         (they're in the tx calldata, on-chain forever). Forward security         says: that's fine, because they're useless to forge future calls.         This test proves the property mechanically:         1. Perform 3 legitimate upgrades (chain advances to position 3)         2. Capture preimages used at positions 0, 1, 2 (publicly visible)         3. Attempt to use any of those past preimages to upgrade now         4. All attempts fail with InvalidChainPreimage"}},"title":"HMACGuardedUUPS Comprehensive Test Suite","version":1},"userdoc":{"events":{"AuthSuccess(uint256,address)":{"notice":"Emitted when an authenticated upgrade is successfully authorized."}},"kind":"user","methods":{"excludeArtifacts()":{"notice":"Returns artifact identifiers configured via `excludeArtifact`."},"excludeContracts()":{"notice":"Returns contract addresses configured via `excludeContract`."},"excludeSelectors()":{"notice":"Returns selector exclusions configured via `excludeSelector`."},"excludeSenders()":{"notice":"Returns senders configured via `excludeSender`."},"failed()":{"notice":"Returns true if any test assertion has failed."},"targetArtifactSelectors()":{"notice":"Returns artifact-selector targets configured via `targetArtifactSelector`."},"targetArtifacts()":{"notice":"Returns artifact identifiers configured via `targetArtifact`."},"targetContracts()":{"notice":"Returns contract addresses configured via `targetContract`."},"targetInterfaces()":{"notice":"Returns address-interface targets configured via `targetInterface`."},"targetSelectors()":{"notice":"Returns selector targets configured via `targetSelector`."},"targetSenders()":{"notice":"Returns sender allowlist configured via `targetSender` (empty means no sender allowlist)."},"test_ForwardSecurity_FullCompromiseAllowsCurrentUpgrade()":{"notice":"Companion test: the chain seed is the only thing an attacker needs.         If we give the attacker the seed (i.e., they steal everything),         they CAN upgrade. This isn't a defense failure \u2014 it's the         intentional trust boundary. Our claim is forward security         (past safety despite future compromise), not unconditional         immunity."},"test_ForwardSecurity_PastPreimagesUselessAfterAdvance()":{"notice":"Forward security demonstration."}},"notice":"Validates the forward-secure HMAC authentication layer for UUPS         upgrades. Tests cover initialization, the happy path, every category         of attack we want to defend against, state integrity after failures,         gas pricing edge cases, and end-to-end multi-step chain returns.excludedArtifacts_: The list of excluded artifact identifiers.

returns.excludedContracts_: The list of excluded contract addresses.

returns.excludedSelectors_: The list of excluded selector configurations.

returns.excludedSenders_: The list of excluded sender addresses.

returns._0: True if any assertion has failed, false otherwise.

returns.targetedArtifactSelectors_: The list of targeted artifact-selector configurations.

returns.targetedArtifacts_: The list of targeted artifact identifiers.

returns.targetedContracts_: The list of targeted contract addresses.

returns.targetedInterfaces_: The list of targeted address-interface configurations.

returns.targetedSelectors_: The list of targeted selector configurations.

returns.targetedSenders_: The list of targeted sender addresses.

test_ForwardSecurity_FullCompromiseAllow.details: This test isn't a security demonstration — it's a clarity         demonstration. It explicitly maps out what the defense does         and doesn't claim.

test_ForwardSecurity_PastPreimagesUseles.details: The cryptographic claim: an attacker who later obtains a chain         preimage cannot use it to forge an upgrade if the chain has         already advanced past that preimage's position.         Past preimages are PUBLICLY VISIBLE after a successful upgrade         (they're in the tx calldata, on-chain forever). Forward security         says: that's fine, because they're useless to forge future calls.         This test proves the property mechanically:         1. Perform 3 legitimate upgrades (chain advances to position 3)         2. Capture preimages used at positions 0, 1, 2 (publicly visible)         3. Attempt to use any of those past preimages to upgrade now         4. All attempts fail with InvalidChainPreimage

failed().notice: Returns true if any test assertion has failed.

targetSenders().notice: Returns sender allowlist configured via `targetSender` (empty means no sender allowlist).

test_ForwardSecurity_FullCompromiseAllow.notice: Companion test: the chain seed is the only thing an attacker needs.         If we give the attacker the seed (i.e., they steal everything),         they CAN upgrade. This isn't a defense failure — it's the         intentional trust boundary. Our claim is forward security         (past safety despite future compromise), not unconditional         immunity.
